channel_topic: Mind-blowing science and nature facts that most people have never heard of. Each video reveals one shocking truth about animals, space, the human body, physics, or Earth in under 45 seconds. Science That Feels Impossible — where reality is stranger than fiction.
youtube_channel_topic: Mind-blowing science and nature facts that most people have never heard of. Each video reveals one shocking truth about animals, space, the human body, physics, or Earth in under 45 seconds. Science That Feels Impossible — where reality is stranger than fiction.
instagram_channel_topic: Educational perfumery and fragrance ingredients. Each 30-second video teaches ONE specific ingredient used in perfume making — its origin, scent profile, extraction method, and how perfumers use it. Topics include: essential oils (rose, jasmine, oud, sandalwood, bergamot, neroli), aroma chemicals (linalool, iso e super, ambroxan, hedione), base notes (musk, amber, vetiver, patchouli), heart notes (ylang ylang, iris, violet), top notes (citrus, aldehydes, green notes), natural resins (labdanum, benzoin, myrrh, frankincense), animal notes (civet, castoreum, ambergris), and extraction methods (steam distillation, enfleurage, CO2 extraction, solvent extraction). Each video shows the raw ingredient, explains its chemistry simply, and tells its story from nature to bottle.
instagram_channel_brief:
INSTAGRAM PERFUMERY EDUCATION RULES:
Each 45-second video teaches ONE perfumery ingredient.

VIDEO STRUCTURE:
Clip 1 (15s):
  Show raw ingredient (flower, wood, resin, chemical bottle)
  Presenter says: 'Today we explore [INGREDIENT NAME]'
  'This comes from [ORIGIN] and smells like [SCENT PROFILE]'
  Close-up of ingredient texture and color

Clip 2 (15s):
  Show extraction process or usage in perfumery
  'Perfumers use this as a [top/heart/base] note'
  'Famous perfumes containing this: [examples]'
  Show perfume bottle or blending process

Clip 3 (15s):
  Fun fact that amazes viewers
  'Did you know: [FUN FACT]'
  'This is why [INGREDIENT] makes perfumes last longer/smell better'
  End: 'Follow for daily fragrance secrets! 🌸'

TITLE FORMAT:
- 'The $30,000/kg Ingredient in Your Favorite Perfume'
- 'Why Rose Absolute Costs More Than Gold'
- 'The Chemical That Makes Perfume Last All Day'
- 'This Whale Secretion Made Your Perfume Famous'
- 'The Flower That Only Blooms at Night for Perfumers'

CONTENT RULES:
- NEVER repeat an ingredient
- Always include origin country
- Always include scent description
- Always include at least one famous perfume
- Include one surprising/shocking fun fact
- End with: Follow for daily fragrance secrets!
preferred_topics: Bizarre truths about the human body
